page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors. It features 360 vCPUs mapped to 180 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 2880.0 GB of dedicated system memory, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 26966.77 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 353.41 GB/sec. Its computational capabilities are supported by advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. Single-core performance is strong, though multi-threaded execution represents its primary strength, as evidenced by top-decile scores in Passmark CPU tests, software compilation, and ray tracing.

The hardware profile delivers high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that scale horizontally across massive memory footprints without requiring local NVMe drives. It achieves top-tier throughput in database operations, Redis SET operations at 412.12 million operations per second, and static web serving. Additionally, the platform shows strong efficiency in large language model inference, particularly for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models.
This server is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching tiers, heavy software compilation pipelines, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 360 vCPUs across 180 physical cores and is equipped with 2880.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the server achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with uncached large block reads reaching 709.69 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-decile results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and prompt processing for large language models. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, heavy software compilation, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360 is a memory-optimized server featuring 360 vCPUs, 2880 GB of RAM, and AMD EPYC 9B14 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360 is a memory-optimized server with 360 vCPUs and 2880 GB RAM, powered by AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 360 vCPUs and 2880 GB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 360 vCPUs and 180 physical cores
Equipped with 2880 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and static web serving
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
